skill.name: simplify
skill.description: Simplify and refine recently modified code for clarity and consistency. Use after writing code to improve readability without changing functionality.
skill.summary: Refines recently changed code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving behavior.
skill.author: brianlovin

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives from Markdown text, prose, and inline code formatting rather than executable behavior. No scripts, imports, shell execution, cryptography, system reconnaissance, or real network calls were found in SKILL.md. The residual risk is low because the skill asks the assistant to proactively refine recently modified code.

low_findings.title: Autonomous Editing Instruction Requires User Control
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to operate autonomously and proactively on recently modified code. This is not malicious, but users should review edits before accepting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly described in the skill text. It is a workflow risk rather than a code execution or data exfiltration risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Backtick Text
dangerous_patterns.description: The external command finding at line 15 is a false positive. The line contains Markdown inline code for the word function, not shell or Ruby backtick execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a bullet about coding style. There is no executable file or command invocation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Documentation URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The hardcoded URL finding at line 12 is a false positive for network activity. It references project standards in prose and does not make an HTTP request.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in Markdown guidance. No request API, import, or tool call is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: No Python HTTP Library
dangerous_patterns.description: The network finding at line 51 is a false positive. The line is prose about operating proactively and contains no Python HTTP library usage.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is Markdown guidance only. The cited line contains no import, library name, or network destination.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Front Matter Text
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at line 3 is a false positive. The line is a natural language description and contains no cryptographic operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The context is YAML front matter metadata. There is no hash, cipher, password handling, or crypto API.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Plain Refactoring Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at line 27 is a false positive. The line discusses removing obvious comments and has no security algorithm.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a readability guideline. No cryptographic primitive or security-sensitive code appears.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Error Handling Prose
dangerous_patterns.description: The system reconnaissance finding at line 18 is a false positive. The line describes error handling style and does not inspect the host system.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The text is a coding-standard bullet. It does not mention commands, filesystem enumeration, environment data, or host metadata.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Ternary Style Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The system reconnaissance finding at line 28 is a false positive. The line advises avoiding nested ternary operators and has no reconnaissance behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a style rule for conditional logic. It has no system inspection intent or capability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Section Heading
dangerous_patterns.description: The system reconnaissance finding at line 31 is a false positive. The line is a section heading about maintaining balance during simplification.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a Markdown numbered heading. It contains no instruction to collect system information.

content.value_statement: Recently changed code can become harder to read after fast implementation work. This skill reviews touched code and applies focused refinements without changing behavior.
actual_capabilities: Reviews recently modified code for simplification opportunities.
Preserves existing behavior while improving structure and clarity.
Applies project coding standards from CLAUDE.md guidance.
Reduces unnecessary nesting, redundancy, and unclear naming.
Balances shorter code against maintainability and debugging needs.
Documents only significant changes that affect understanding.
limitations: Does not define or run tests by itself.
Depends on available project standards being accurate.
Focuses on recently modified code unless asked for broader review.
May propose style changes that still need human review.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Feature Work
use_cases.description: Refine a new feature after implementation so the final diff is easier to review.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Improve naming, structure, and consistency before sending code for team review.

use_cases.title: Apply Local Standards
use_cases.description: Align recently touched TypeScript or React code with project style expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Simplify My Last Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the code I just changed and simplify it for readability. Preserve behavior and explain only meaningful edits.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request after a small implementation task

prompt_templates.title: Clean Up This Diff
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current diff and refine only touched files. Reduce unnecessary nesting, improve names, and keep behavior unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Project Standards
prompt_templates.prompt: Simplify the recently modified code using this repository's conventions. Pay special attention to imports, function declarations, and component props.
prompt_templates.scenario: Matching existing TypeScript or React style

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Refactoring Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the recent changes for over-complicated control flow, redundant abstractions, and unclear boundaries. Make scoped improvements and note tradeoffs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced review before merging a larger change

output_examples.input: A recently added component has repeated condition checks, mixed naming, and extra comments.
output: Simplified the conditional flow into clearer branches.
Renamed temporary values to match their purpose.
Removed comments that repeated the implementation.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds helper functions that duplicate existing logic.
output: Left the existing abstraction in place where it improved readability.

output_examples.input: A modified TypeScript file uses inconsistent top-level function styles.
output: Converted top-level helpers to the preferred function form.
Added explicit return annotations where appropriate.
Sorted imports to match project conventions.

best_practices: Run the skill after implementation and before final review.
Keep the scope limited to recently touched code unless broader cleanup is intended.
Review the final diff to confirm behavior and intent remain unchanged.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to rewrite unrelated stable code during a focused change.
Accepting simplifications that remove useful abstractions or domain clarity.
Replacing tests or functional verification with readability review.

faq.question: Does this skill change program behavior?
faq.answer: It is designed to preserve behavior while improving clarity and consistency.

faq.question: Can it review an entire repository?
faq.answer: Its default scope is recently modified code. Ask explicitly for a broader review.

faq.question: Does it run tests?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on code refinement and should be paired with normal test execution.

faq.question: What languages does it target?
faq.answer: The guidance is strongest for TypeScript and React patterns, but the principles are general.

faq.question: How does it use project standards?
faq.answer: It follows repository conventions referenced by the skill, including import style and function patterns.

faq.question: Is it safe for production code?
faq.answer: Yes, when users review the diff and run the normal validation steps before merging.
